Module com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java
Package com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.internal.scope

Interface GlobalConfiguration

All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractGlobalConfiguration, MainGlobalConfiguration
public interface GlobalConfiguration
The global configuration inherited by all verifiers.

Note: Verifiers inherit from the global configuration at instantiation time. Their configuration is not affected by subsequent changes to the global configuration.

However, updating settings not found in Configuration (such as withTerminalEncoding(TerminalEncoding)) will impact the behavior of existing verifiers. Users are expected to update these settings at startup, or take active measures to ensure that verifiers are idle while the configuration is being updated. Failure to do so may result in undesirable behavior such as verifiers outputting ANSI colors to a terminal that is no longer configured to render them.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type Method Description
    TerminalEncoding getTerminalEncoding()
    Returns the current terminal encoding.
    int getTerminalWidth()
    Returns the width of the terminal.
    boolean isCleanStackTrace()
    Indicates if exception stack traces should omit references to this library.
    boolean isDiffEnabled()
    Indicates if exceptions should show the difference between the actual and expected values.
    Set<TerminalEncoding> listTerminalEncodings()
    Returns the encodings supported by the terminal.
    GlobalConfiguration withCleanStackTrace()
    Indicates that exception stack traces should omit references to this library.
    GlobalConfiguration withDefaultTerminalEncoding()
    Indicates that the terminal encoding should be auto-detected.
    GlobalConfiguration withDefaultTerminalWidth()
    Indicates that the terminal width should be auto-detected.
    GlobalConfiguration withDiff()
    Indicates that exceptions should show the difference between the actual and expected values.
    GlobalConfiguration withoutCleanStackTrace()
    Indicates that exception stack traces should contain references to this library.
    GlobalConfiguration withoutDiff()
    Indicates that exceptions should not show the difference between the actual and expected values.
    GlobalConfiguration withTerminalEncoding​(TerminalEncoding encoding)
    Indicates the type of encoding that the terminal supports.
    GlobalConfiguration withTerminalWidth​(int width)
    Indicates the width of the terminal.

  • Method Details

    • listTerminalEncodings

      Set<TerminalEncoding> listTerminalEncodings()
      Returns the encodings supported by the terminal.
      Returns:
      the encodings supported by the terminal
      See Also:
      withTerminalEncoding(TerminalEncoding), withDefaultTerminalEncoding()

    • getTerminalEncoding

      TerminalEncoding getTerminalEncoding()
      Returns the current terminal encoding.
      Returns:
      the current terminal encoding (defaults to the auto-detected encoding)

    • withDefaultTerminalEncoding

      GlobalConfiguration withDefaultTerminalEncoding()
      Indicates that the terminal encoding should be auto-detected.
      Returns:
      this
      See Also:
      withTerminalEncoding(TerminalEncoding)

    • withTerminalEncoding

      GlobalConfiguration withTerminalEncoding​(TerminalEncoding encoding)
      Indicates the type of encoding that the terminal supports.

      This feature can be used to force the use of ANSI colors even when their support is not detected.

      Parameters:
      encoding - the type of encoding that the terminal supports
      Returns:
      this
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if encoding is null
      See Also:
      withDefaultTerminalEncoding()

    • getTerminalWidth

      int getTerminalWidth()
      Returns the width of the terminal.
      Returns:
      the width of the terminal in characters (defaults to the auto-detected width)

    • withDefaultTerminalWidth

      GlobalConfiguration withDefaultTerminalWidth()
      Indicates that the terminal width should be auto-detected. If the width cannot be auto-detected, a value of 80 is used.
      Returns:
      this
      See Also:
      withTerminalWidth(int)

    • withTerminalWidth

      GlobalConfiguration withTerminalWidth​(int width)
      Indicates the width of the terminal.

      This feature can be used to override the default terminal width when it cannot be auto-detected.

      Parameters:
      width - the width of the terminal in characters
      Returns:
      this
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if width is zero or negative
      See Also:
      withDefaultTerminalWidth()

    • isCleanStackTrace

      boolean isCleanStackTrace()
      Indicates if exception stack traces should omit references to this library.
      Returns:
      true by default
      See Also:
      withCleanStackTrace(), withoutCleanStackTrace()

    • withCleanStackTrace

      GlobalConfiguration withCleanStackTrace()
      Indicates that exception stack traces should omit references to this library.
      Returns:
      this
      See Also:
      isCleanStackTrace()

    • withoutCleanStackTrace

      GlobalConfiguration withoutCleanStackTrace()
      Indicates that exception stack traces should contain references to this library.
      Returns:
      this
      See Also:
      isCleanStackTrace()

    • isDiffEnabled

      boolean isDiffEnabled()
      Indicates if exceptions should show the difference between the actual and expected values.
      Returns:
      true by default
      See Also:
      withDiff(), withoutDiff()

    • withDiff

      GlobalConfiguration withDiff()
      Indicates that exceptions should show the difference between the actual and expected values.
      Returns:
      this
      See Also:
      isDiffEnabled()

    • withoutDiff

      GlobalConfiguration withoutDiff()
      Indicates that exceptions should not show the difference between the actual and expected values.
      Returns:
      this
      See Also:
      isDiffEnabled()
